summary: Review-2 covers the external-review-improvement diff (85b25b30..HEAD): four files. The substantive change is the deletion of tests/unit/deploy/push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh (170 lines) to satisfy external reviewer gndrmnn, who stated the file 'serves no relevant purpose' because it tests 'a feature that was never added or was removed during the Keystone admin re-bootstrap work'. That premise is factually incorrect: deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl (a live CC-0109 / REQ-008 deliverable) exists and is correctly scoped, and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh:102 still binds it on the management branch. The deleted test ran green (23/23 PASS) against the current tree and was the ONLY automated regression guard for the CC-0083 audit invariant (eso-management stays read-only; admin write confined to the two literal bootstrap/keystone-admin data+metadata leaves with no wildcard; binding scoped to the management role) — exactly the documented BLOCKER criterion and Task 4.1's 'add a shell unit test asserting scope + binding'. Deleting it leaves REQ-008's test acceptance criterion unmet and removes the only guard against a future edit silently adding a write wildcard or granting write to eso-management. The other three files are metadata/doc updates; the review_patterns occurrence appended for CC-0109 both mischaracterizes the test as orphaned and is mis-filed under a pattern about test-only code embedded in production .go files (an unrelated subject). I acknowledge the external-review context and am not unilaterally reverting; I am surfacing the factual conflict for human adjudication.

tests_checklist.item: Tests for in-scope changes exist and pass

code_quality_checklist.item: Pre-deletion rename to the *_test.sh convention was correct
review_patterns occurrence matches the pattern's stated subject / detection-hint
Change scope limited to the four listed files

security_checklist.item: push-keystone-admin.hcl still scoped to exactly two literal admin leaves (no wildcard/+)
Automated regression guard for the above least-privilege invariants is present

architecture_checklist.item: No production code or CRD/spec touched in this diff

fail_fast_checklist.item: N/A — no runtime code changed in this diff

defensive_checklist.item: N/A — no input-handling code changed in this diff

security_findings.description: The deletion removed the only automated regression guard for the CC-0083 audit invariant. push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh asserted: (1) push-keystone-admin.hcl grants create/update/read/delete on exactly kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin and kv-v2/metadata/bootstrap/keystone-admin with NO wildcard or '+' glob; (2) eso-management.hcl grants no create/update/delete on the bootstrap subtree (read-only invariant); (3) setup-auth.sh appends ',push-keystone-admin' ONLY inside the management-cluster branch and nowhere else. The policy and binding still exist and are currently correct, but with the test gone, a future edit that adds a trailing '*' to the policy path, grants write to eso-management, or binds the policy to a non-management cluster would now pass CI unnoticed — silently escalating a single-credential rotation grant into write access over the whole bootstrap subtree (e.g. database root credentials).
security_findings.fix: Restore the test (under its renamed path push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh). It passes 23/23 against the current tree. If the external reviewer's underlying concern was test value or location, reconcile that without dropping the invariant assertions — e.g. confirm with gndrmnn given that deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl is a live CC-0109/REQ-008 deliverable, not an orphan.

issues_found.description: External reviewer gndrmnn requested deleting tests/unit/deploy/push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh on the stated premise that it tests 'a feature that was never added or was removed during the Keystone admin re-bootstrap work'. That premise is factually wrong. deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl exists (added by CC-0109 commit 680717f1, currently in the tree) and is the exact artifact REQ-008 / Task 4.1 require, and setup-auth.sh:102 still appends ',push-keystone-admin' on the management branch. The deleted test was a working, meaningful regression guard (23 assertions, all PASS against the current tree) and Task 4.1 explicitly required it ('Add a shell unit test under tests/unit/deploy/ asserting scope + binding'). Deleting it leaves that acceptance criterion unmet and removes coverage of a documented BLOCKER security invariant. NOTE: this change was made to address external feedback (external-review protection); I am flagging the factual contradiction and the resulting coverage gap for human adjudication rather than asserting the deletion was careless.
issues_found.fix: Restore the test (renamed form push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh, which already aligns with the tests/unit/deploy/*_test.sh convention used by chaos_mesh_overlay_test.sh, flux_system_fluxinstance_test.sh, etc.). If gndrmnn intended a different (genuinely orphaned) file, confirm — but the policy + binding this file guards are live CC-0109 deliverables, so the security/least-privilege assertions must be retained somewhere.
issues_found.evidence: ls shows deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl (3509 bytes, present). `grep -n push-keystone-admin deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh` -> line 102: token_policies+=",push-keystone-admin". Running the deleted blob from tests/unit/deploy/ -> 'Results: 23 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped' (exit 0). `grep -rl push-keystone-admin tests/` after deletion -> (none).

issues_found.description: The CC-0109 occurrence appended to .planwerk/review_patterns/keep-test-only-code-out-of-production-files.md (occurrences bumped 1 -> 2) is (a) factually inaccurate — it describes the deleted test as 'testing a feature that was never added or was removed', but deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl is still in the codebase — and (b) mis-filed: this pattern's subject and detection-hint are about test-only constructors/helpers embedded in non-_test.go PRODUCTION source files (e.g. newXxxForTest in collectors.go), not about deleting an allegedly-orphaned standalone shell test. Recording a non-matching case here inflates the pattern's apparent frequency and will mislead future reviews into deleting live config-assertion tests.
issues_found.fix: Either remove the CC-0109 entry (and revert occurrences to 1) or relocate it to a pattern about orphaned/irrelevant tests and correct the rationale to reflect that the policy still exists. Do not file it under 'test-only code in production files'.
issues_found.evidence: Pattern Detection-Hint: 'Scan non-_test.go files for constructors, helpers, or methods whose only callers are in _test.go files'. Appended entry: 'Fix: Deleted the orphaned test file tests/unit/deploy/test_push_keystone_admin_policy.sh entirely.' — but push-keystone-admin.hcl exists and setup-auth.sh:102 binds it.

suggested_improvements: When an external reviewer recommends deleting a test, verify the artifact under test still exists before deleting — here a one-line `test -f deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-admin.hcl` would have shown the policy is live.

If the team decides the HCL-string-assertion style is genuinely low-value, replace it with equivalent coverage (e.g. an e2e/Chainsaw assertion that the management ESO role can write only the two admin leaves) rather than dropping the invariant entirely.
next_steps: Adjudicate the deletion: restore push_keystone_admin_policy_test.sh (it passes 23/23 and satisfies REQ-008/Task 4.1) OR document an explicit, reviewer-confirmed decision that the policy's least-privilege invariant will be guarded elsewhere.
Correct or remove the CC-0109 occurrence in keep-test-only-code-out-of-production-files.md so it does not seed future false-orphan deletions.
